ingredients.name: Hakuba Ski Jumping Stadium
ingredients.entityContext: The Hakuba ski jump facility built for the 1998 Nagano Winter Olympics appears in Episode 9 (and in the series opener), where the Club films the towering in-run ramp against the Northern Alps backdrop.

ingredients.name: Tenka Chaya
ingredients.address: Tenka Chaya, 2739 Kawaguchi, 富士河口湖町 Fujikawaguchiko, Minamitsuru District, Yamanashi 401-0304, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The rustic mountain teahouse near Kawaguchiko appears in the mono opening sequence, establishing the Cinephoto Club's Yamanashi stomping ground and nodding to its famous connection to the Yuru Camp anime.

ingredients.name: Aqua Athletic
ingredients.entityContext: The lakeside athletic facility appears in the mono opening sequence as the Club cycles and explores the Fuji Five Lakes area around their home base.

ingredients.name: Nagatoro Bridge
ingredients.address: Nagatoro Bridge, Nagatoro, Saku, Nagano 385-0021, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This bridge in Nagano appears in the mono opening sequence as one of the scenic country road shots that frame the Cinephoto Club's regional adventures.

ingredients.name: Arakurayama Sengen Park
ingredients.address: Arakurayama Sengen Park, 2-chome-4-1 Asama, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi 403-0011, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The park's five-storied pagoda framed against Mt. Fuji is the signature image of the mono opening, serving as the visual emblem of the Cinephoto Club's home prefecture.

ingredients.name: Path of Cherry Blossoms Stone Monument
ingredients.address: Path of Cherry Blossoms Stone Monument, Tokojimachi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0808, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 1 Satsuki and the Club pass this cherry-blossom-lined trail near Kofu, capturing it with their action cameras as part of their early local exploration.

ingredients.name: Yamanashi Prefectural Science Museum
ingredients.address: Yamanashi Prefectural Science Museum, 358-1 Atagomachi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The science museum in Atago Park appears in Episode 1 as the Cinephoto Club scouts filming locations around Kofu for their first project together.

ingredients.name: Yamanashi Prefectural Science Center Observation Deck
ingredients.localName: 山梨県立科学館展望デッキ
ingredients.address: Yamanashi Prefectural Science Center Observation Deck, 358-1 Atagomachi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The observation deck of Yamanashi's science center features in Episode 1, giving the Club an elevated view over Kofu that they use as a cinematic establishing shot.

ingredients.name: Kofu Castle Blacksmith Enclosure Ruins
ingredients.localName: 甲府城 鍛冶曲輪跡
ingredients.address: Kofu Castle Blacksmith Enclosure Ruins, 1 Chome-5 Marunouchi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The old castle district ruins near central Kofu Station are visited in Episode 2 as the Club explores their home city for atmospheric shooting spots.

ingredients.name: Sakashita Gate Stone Walls
ingredients.address: Sakashita Gate, 1-1 Chiyoda, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 100-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Kofu Castle Tetsumon Gate
ingredients.address: Kofu Castle Tetsumon Gate, 1 Chome-5 Marunouchi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kofu Castle's iron gate appears in Episode 2 when the Club films around the historic Marunouchi area of Kofu, drawn by its samurai-era stone walls.

ingredients.name: Kofu Castle Ruins
ingredients.address: Kōfu Castle Ruins, 1 Chome-5-4 Marunouchi, Kofu, Yamanashi 400-0031, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The main grounds of Kofu Castle are visited in Episode 2, with the Club filming the stone-walled hilltop ruins that overlook the city basin.

ingredients.name: Yuki Bridge
ingredients.address: Yuki Bridge, Yūki, Yuki, Ibaraki 307-0001, Japan

ingredients.name: Suntama IC
ingredients.address: Tama Station, 3-chōme Momijigaoka, Fuchu, Tokyo 183-0004, Japan

ingredients.name: Daily Yamazaki Hokuto Sudama Inter
ingredients.localName: デイリーヤマザキ 北杜須玉インター店
ingredients.address: Daily Yamazaki Hokuto Sudama Inter, 1910-1 Sutamacho Wakamiko, Hokuto, Yamanashi 408-0112, Japan

ingredients.name: Shichiken
ingredients.localName: 七賢（山梨銘醸株式会社）
ingredients.entityContext: The historic Shichiken sake brewery in Hakushu appears in Episode 2 during the Club's drive through the Hakushu whisky valley, stopping to admire traditional Koshu brewing culture.

ingredients.name: Old Koshu-kaido
ingredients.entityContext: The old Koshu highway in Hakushu appears in Episode 2 as a scenic rural road that the Club drives and films on their way north through Yamanashi.

ingredients.name: Makiba Park
ingredients.localName: 山梨県立 まきば公園
ingredients.entityContext: The highland pasture park with views of the Southern Alps appears in Episode 2, providing the Club with wide open grassland scenery for their footage.

ingredients.name: Makiba Koen Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: まきば公園 駐車場

ingredients.name: Higashizawa Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The Higashizawa Bridge near Kiyosato appears in Episode 2 as a roadside landmark during the Club's drive through the Yatsugatake highlands.

ingredients.name: Nichinoh Nobeyama Office
ingredients.localName: ニチノー 野辺山事務所

ingredients.name: Koumi Furusato Cafe
ingredients.localName: レストハウスふるさと

ingredients.name: Hinata Koba observatory
ingredients.entityContext: This mountaintop viewpoint overlooking the Yatsugatake range and Southern Alps features in Episode 2, giving the Club panoramic highland footage.

ingredients.name: Motosuko Observation Park
ingredients.entityContext: The observation point overlooking Lake Motosu with Mt. Fuji behind is visited in Episode 3 as one of the Club's Yuru Camp pilgrimage stops, recreating the serene lakeside view from the original camping anime.

ingredients.name: Motosuko Lodge Zen Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 本栖湖ロッジ前 駐車場
ingredients.entityContext: The parking area at Lake Motosu features in Episode 3 as a staging point for the Cinephoto Club's Yuru Camp pilgrimage around the lakeside campsites.

ingredients.name: Koan Main Building Central Lodge
ingredients.localName: 浩庵本館セントラルロッジ
ingredients.entityContext: The Kosoan lodge on Lake Motosu appears in Episode 3 during the Club's Yuru Camp pilgrimage; An comically falls asleep at the same spot where Nadeshiko napped in Yuru Camp, in a scene unique to the anime adaptation.

ingredients.name: Fumotoppara Campground
ingredients.localName: ふもとっぱら(キャンプサイト)
ingredients.entityContext: In Episode 3 the Cinephoto Club visits this iconic campground at the base of Mt. Fuji as part of their Yuru Camp pilgrimage, recreating the same vast open field view that made Rin's solo camp so memorable.

ingredients.name: Tire Garden Nambu
ingredients.localName: タイヤガーデン 南部店

ingredients.name: Shibireko Suimeisou
ingredients.entityContext: The lakeside lodge at Lake Shibire appears in Episode 3 as one of the Club's pilgrimage stops through Yamanashi's highland lakes.

ingredients.name: Yashajin Hutte
ingredients.localName: 夜叉神ヒュッテ（旧夜叉神の森）

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Minami-Alps Yagoshima
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 南アルプス野牛島店

ingredients.name: Suwako Service area (Northbound)
ingredients.localName: 諏訪湖 サービスエリア (下り)
ingredients.entityContext: The Lake Suwa expressway service area is a rest stop in Episode 3 during the Club's frantic overnight road trip through Nagano on the Yuru Camp pilgrimage.

ingredients.name: Matsumoto-jo Castle
ingredients.entityContext: Matsumoto Castle appears in Episode 3 when the Club detours through Nagano during their pilgrimage road trip, filming the iconic black keep reflected in the castle moat.

ingredients.name: Jinbagatayama Campground
ingredients.entityContext: Jinbagatayama campground in the Central Alps appears in Episode 3 as part of the Club's wide-ranging camping-site pilgrimage, prized for its panoramic views of the Ina Valley.

ingredients.name: Suganodai Bus Center Parking Lot
ingredients.localName: 菅の台バスセンター 駐車場
ingredients.entityContext: The Suganodai bus hub near Sengokudaira appears in Episode 3 as the Club's departure point for the ropeway up into the Kiso Valley highlands.

ingredients.name: Three gates of Kouzenji Temple
ingredients.entityContext: The triple gate of Kozenji Temple is shown in Episode 3 as the Club walks through the mossy cedar-lined approach to this Nagano mountain temple.

ingredients.name: Hoshakuzan Kozenji Temple
ingredients.entityContext: Kozenji Temple's main hall in Komagane appears in Episode 3 as a quiet cultural stop on the pilgrimage circuit through the Central Alps foothills.

ingredients.name: Takabocchi plateau
ingredients.entityContext: The highland Takabocchi plateau in Nagano is visited in Episode 3, giving the Club sweeping views of Lake Suwa and the surrounding mountains that echo the scenic vistas of Yuru Camp.

ingredients.name: Katakurakan
ingredients.entityContext: The historic Katakurakan bathhouse in Suwa appears in Episode 3 during the Club's overnight journey through the pilgrimage route.

ingredients.name: Kirigamine Venus
ingredients.entityContext: The Kirigamine highland grasslands near Suwa appear in Episode 3, where the Club films the rolling hills of the Venus Line road that cuts through the plateau.

ingredients.name: Budokoubou Wine Glass Museum
ingredients.localName: 葡萄工房ワイングラス館

ingredients.name: Katsunuma Budou no Oka
ingredients.localName: 甲州市勝沼 ぶどうの丘
ingredients.entityContext: The Budou no Oka wine complex in Katsunuma appears in Episode 4 when the Club explores the Yamanashi wine country, visiting the hilltop wine cave for a tasting session.

ingredients.name: Budou no Oka Natural Onsen Tenku-no-yu
ingredients.entityContext: The rooftop onsen at Budou no Oka overlooking the Katsunuma vineyards features in Episode 4, where the Club soaks in the open-air bath after their winery tour.

ingredients.localName: いちやまマート 山梨店

ingredients.name: Statue of Takeda Shingen
ingredients.entityContext: The famous bronze statue of warlord Takeda Shingen in front of Kofu Station appears in Episode 4 as a local landmark the Club passes while exploring central Kofu.

ingredients.name: Hottarakashi Onsen
ingredients.entityContext: The famous cliffside onsen overlooking Kofu Basin and Mt. Fuji appears in Episode 6, where the Club enjoys the outdoor bath at sunrise and films the panoramic view.

Kuon-ji Sanmon

ingredients.name: Norikura Tatamidaira
ingredients.entityContext: The high-altitude Tatamidaira terminal on Mt. Norikura appears in Episode 8 as the Club rides the bus up to the alpine zone, stepping out into thin air and vast volcanic rock scenery.

ingredients.name: Mt. Maou
ingredients.entityContext: The volcanic peak of Mt. Maou on the Norikura skyline appears in Episode 8 as the Club hikes up to the summit crater for a bird's-eye view over the Northern Alps.

ingredients.name: Kamikochi
ingredients.entityContext: The famous alpine valley of Kamikochi appears in Episode 9 as the Club enters the protected highland basin, filming the Azusa River and the Hotaka peaks for their grandest location shoot yet.

ingredients.entityContext: The dam-top observation deck at Kurobe appears in Episode 9, where the Club gathers to look down at the summer discharge spray and out across the alpine peaks.

ingredients.name: Kurobe Dam
ingredients.entityContext: Japan's tallest dam at 186 m appears in Episode 9 as a dramatic finale location, with the Club filming the massive arch dam and its turquoise reservoir set among the Northern Alps.

ingredients.name: Hakuba Ski Jumping Stadium
ingredients.entityContext: The Hakuba ski jump facility built for the 1998 Nagano Winter Olympics appears in Episode 9 (and in the series opener), where the Club films the towering in-run ramp against the Northern Alps backdrop.

ingredients.name: Togakushi Shrine

ingredients.entityContext: The Shingen Mochi sweets factory in Kofu appears in Episode 11, where the Club tours the production line of Yamanashi's most famous wagashi and stocks up on the kinako-covered mochi.
